name: Hike from Trillo — circular route along the Tajo River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir
name_original: Budia desde Trillo — circular por el río Tajo y el Pantano de Entrepeñas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Trillo navigate a landscape shaped by the Tajo River and its tributaries, featuring river valleys and the expansive Entrepeñas Reservoir. The terrain includes varied elevations, with some routes offering significant climbs and descents. This area provides a mix of open stretches and paths that follow natural watercourses. Best touring cycling routes around Trillo The most popular touring cycling route is Hike from Trillo — circular route along the Tajo River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir, a 44.5 miles (71.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the Tajo River and the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cifuentes from Trillo — circular by the Camino del río Cifuentes, a moderate 19.6 miles (31.5 km) path. This route follows the Camino del río Cifuentes, providing riverside scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Trillo – Cifuentes River Waterfall loop from Trillo, a 22.0 miles (35.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Trillo is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and rolling hills. The network offers options for both moderate and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Trillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Trillo?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Trillo area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Trillo?
A: While many routes in Trillo feature varied elevations, some moderate options are suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188378009/cifuentes-from-trillo-circular-by-the-camino-del-rio-cifuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes from Trillo — circular by the Camino del río Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.6-mile path that follows riverside scenery.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Trillo?
A: The terrain around Trillo is characterized by river valleys, especially along the Tajo River and its tributaries, and views of the expansive Entrepeñas Reservoir. You'll encounter varied elevations, including rolling hills, with some routes featuring significant climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Trillo?
A: Yes, several routes in Trillo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188340574/hike-from-trillo-circular-route-along-the-tajo-river-and-the-entrepenas-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Trillo — circular route along the Tajo River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes in Trillo often pass by notable natural and historical sites. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or see the unique rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5610581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Arco&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant landmark.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Trillo offers several difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam – View of Alocén loop from Durón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 46.9-mile route with substantial climbs, perfect for experienced riders seeking a demanding ride.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Trillo?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Trillo, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters can be cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling, particularly on sunny days.

Q: Can I find routes that specifically feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo – Cifuentes River Waterfall loop from Trillo&lt;/a&gt; is a 22.0-mile trail that specifically leads through varied terrain to the Cifuentes River Waterfall.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Trillo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to reservoir views, and the mix of moderate and challenging options available.

Q: Are there any shorter touring cycling routes for a quick ride?
A: For a shorter ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall – Trillo loop from Trillo&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route is 17.2 miles long and can typically be completed in under 2 hours, offering a good option for a quicker outing.
tips: Magnificent arched viaduct built over the Tagus River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir.
Budia is a cute little town nestled in the plains of the Alcarria and the Tagus valley. The town became famous in the book "Journey to the Alcarria" by Camilo José Cela.

The gravel trip continues along rural roads to Trillo, on the banks of the Tagus River.

The streets of the town are on a staggered slope and the Cifuentes River crosses the center of the town, creating several beautiful waterfalls, an extraordinary wonder of nature in the peasant environment of the Alcarreño mountains and valleys.

In the center of the town it is inevitable to stop at the terrace of the Gastrobar Pura and enjoy the spectacle of the water while eating a plate of local specialties.

Trillo is also the headquarters of Zona Zentro, a space focused on discovering the values of the territory through the bicycle. The route leaves the refreshing company of the waterfalls of the Cifuentes River by the monumental Gothic bridge of the Tagus and undertakes a seductive journey through the meanders of the Tagus at the end of the Entrepeñas reservoir.
